prompt_text: Give a weekly diet and workout routine.
response_text:
Here is a comprehensive weekly diet and workout routine designed for general health, fat loss, and muscle maintenance. This plan is balanced, sustainable, and suitable for most fitness levels. **Important Disclaimer:** Consult a doctor before starting any new diet or exercise program, especially if you have any pre-existing health conditions. --- ### Part 1: Weekly Workout Routine **Frequency:** 4 days of strength training, 2 days of active recovery/cardio, 1 full rest day. **Key Principles:** - **Progressive Overload:** Each week, try to lift slightly heavier weight, do one more rep, or one more set. - **Form First:** Perfect your technique before increasing weight. - **Warm-up (5-10 mins):** Dynamic stretches (leg swings, arm circles, torso twists) and light cardio (jumping jacks, jogging in place). - **Cool-down (5 mins):** Static stretching (hold each stretch for 20-30 seconds). | Day | Workout Type | Exercises (3 sets of 8-12 reps unless noted) | Cardio (Optional) | | :-- | :--- | :--- | :--- | | **Monday** | **Push Day** (Chest, Shoulders, Triceps) | 1. Barbell or Dumbbell Bench Press
2. Overhead Press (Dumbbell or Barbell)
3. Incline Dumbbell Press
4. Lateral Raises
5. Triceps Pushdowns (Cable or Band) | 15-20 min LISS (brisk walk on treadmill, incline walk) | | **Tuesday** | **Pull Day** (Back, Biceps, Rear Delts) | 1. Pull-ups (or Lat Pulldowns)
2. Bent-Over Rows (Barbell or Dumbbell)
3. Seated Cable Rows
4. Face Pulls (for rear delts)
5. Dumbbell Bicep Curls | 15-20 min LISS | | **Wednesday**| **Active Recovery / Cardio** | Low-impact activity: 30-45 min brisk walking, cycling, swimming, or yoga. | Focus on heart rate zone 1-2 (conversational pace). | | **Thursday** | **Leg Day** (Quads, Hamstrings, Glutes, Calves) | 1. Goblet Squats or Barbell Squats
2. Romanian Deadlifts (Dumbbell or Barbell)
3. Walking Lunges (or Reverse Lunges)
4. Leg Press or Hip Thrusts
5. Calf Raises (standing or seated) | 15-20 min LISS | | **Friday** | **Full Body / Core & Accessories** | 1. Deadlifts (Conventional or Sumo) - 3 sets of 5 reps
2. Dumbbell Rows - 3x10
3. Dumbbell Bench Press - 3x10
4. Plank - 3 x 45-60 sec hold
5. Hanging Knee Raises (or Lying Leg Raises) - 3x15 | 20 min HIIT (e.g., 30 sec sprint, 90 sec walk, repeat 8 times) | | **Saturday** | **Active Recovery / Cardio** | Same as Wednesday: 30-45 min of low-intensity activity. | Focus on movement and fun. | | **Sunday** | **Full Rest Day** | No structured exercise. Focus on stretching, foam rolling, and sleep. | Listen to your body. | --- ### Part 2: Weekly Diet Plan (Approx. 1,800-2,000 Calories) **Goal:** High protein, moderate carbs, healthy fats. Adjust portions based on your hunger and activity level. **Key Principles:** - **Protein:** Aim for 1.6-2.2g per kg of body weight (e.g., 80-110g for a 50kg person, 130-175g for an 80kg person). - **Hydration:** Drink 2-3 liters of water daily. - **Whole Foods:** Prioritize lean proteins, whole grains, fruits, vegetables, and healthy fats. - **Flexibility:** This is a template. Swap similar foods (e.g., chicken for tofu, broccoli for spinach). | Day | Breakfast | Lunch | Dinner | Snack (Optional) | | :-- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | | **Monday** | 2 scrambled eggs + 1 slice whole-wheat toast + 1/2 avocado | Grilled chicken salad (5 oz chicken, mixed greens, tomatoes, cucumber, 2 tbsp vinaigrette) | Baked salmon (6 oz) + 1 cup quinoa + roasted asparagus | 1 apple + 1 tbsp peanut butter | | **Tuesday** | Greek yogurt (1 cup) + 1/2 cup berries + 1 tbsp chia seeds | Turkey & veggie wrap (whole-wheat tortilla, 4 oz turkey, lettuce, tomato, mustard) | Lean beef stir-fry (5 oz beef, bell peppers, broccoli, snap peas, 1 cup brown rice) | 1/2 cup cottage cheese + a few almonds | | **Wednesday** | Oatmeal (1/2 cup dry) + 1 scoop protein powder + 1/2 banana | Leftover beef stir-fry | Lentil soup (2 cups) + side salad with 3 oz grilled chicken | Handful of carrots + 2 tbsp hummus | | **Thursday** | Smoothie (1 scoop protein, 1 cup spinach, 1/2 banana, 1 cup almond milk) | Tuna salad (1 can tuna, 1 tbsp mayo, celery, on 2 rice cakes) | Baked chicken breast (6 oz) + 1 sweet potato + steamed green beans | 1 hard-boiled egg + 1 pear | | **Friday** | 2 eggs any style + 1/2 cup black beans + 1/4 avocado | Leftover chicken & sweet potato | Whole-wheat pasta (1 cup cooked) with lean turkey meatballs (4 oz) + marinara sauce + side salad | 1/2 cup Greek yogurt + a few walnuts | | **Saturday** | Protein pancakes (1 scoop protein, 1 egg, 1/2 banana) | Large salad (5 oz grilled shrimp, mixed greens, bell peppers, 2 tbsp light dressing) | Homemade chicken fajitas (5 oz chicken, peppers, onions, 2 small corn tortillas) | Handful of grapes + 1 string cheese | | **Sunday** | 2 slices whole-wheat toast + 1 tbsp almond butter + 1/2 banana | Leftover fajitas | "Bowl" meal: 5 oz grilled tofu or chicken, 1 cup brown rice, roasted veggies (broccoli, carrots), 1 tbsp tahini dressing | 1 cup mixed berries | ### Part 3: Weekly Schedule (Putting It All
